title: Capacity Building for Food Inspectors on Salmonella Risk Assessment
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   with     significant     implications     for     public     health     and     trade  .   Effective     control     requires     trained     food     inspectors     capable     of     performing     risk-based     assessments     across     the     food     chain  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     urgent     need     to     strengthen     inspector     competencies     through     structured     capacity-building     programs  ,   standardized     training     curricula  ,   and     adoption     of   modern   risk     assessment     tools  . By   equipping     inspectors     with     the     skills     to     detect     critical     control     points  ,   evaluate     contamination     risks  ,   and     ensure     regulatory     compliance  ,   countries     can     reduce     Salmonella     outbreaks     and     improve     consumer     trust  .   A     well-trained     workforce     enhances     food     system     resilience  ,   protects     vulnerable     populations  ,   and     contributes     to     sustainable     economic     growth     by     reducing     healthcare     costs     and     minimizing     food     recalls     and     trade     disruptions  .
